qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Cloud Code context prototype pollution via X-Parse-Cloud-Context header
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns unfiltered prototype-pollution keys in the X-Parse-Cloud-Context header and Cloud Code context handling, not RestQuery.js query condition depth validation before the transform pipeline.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing Content Security Policy and critical security headers
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns omitted browser security headers in the HTTP middleware chain, not deeply nested logical query operators bypassing RestQuery.js depth validation.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Uploaded files publicly accessible without authentication or access control
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns missing authorization on file download routes, not the query parser's pre-validation transform-depth bypass.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Regex DoS (ReDoS) via unvalidated $regex operator in queries
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is query-related but describes authenticated use of MongoDB $regex patterns reaching MongoTransform.js, while the advisory root cause is unauthenticated deeply nested $or/$and/$nor conditions recursively processed in RestQuery.js before query depth validation.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Cloud Code execution without timeout or resource limits enables denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns unbounded Cloud Code function execution and event-loop blocking, not query condition nesting depth validation in RestQuery.js.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is a Parse Server RestQuery.js issue where deeply nested $or, $and, or $nor query conditions are recursively processed by the transform pipeline before query depth validation. The closest qualifying finding, redos-via-regex-query, is a distinct authenticated $regex pattern-complexity issue in MongoTransform.js.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-9fjp-q3c4-6w3j describes unauthenticated deeply nested logical query conditions that hang Parse Server by bypassing query depth validation before the transform pipeline.

Official patch commits 2581b5426047ce9cbcd3d9c0e8379e9c30e23ab5 and 85994eff9e7b34cac7e1a2f5791985022a1461d1 add _UnsafeRestQuery.prototype.validateQueryDepth() in src/RestQuery.js before buildRestWhere(), checking $or, $and, and $nor nesting.
redos-via-regex-query cites src/Adapters/Storage/Mongo/MongoTransform.js and unvalidated $regex catastrophic backtracking, with authenticated user access and no nested logical-operator depth bypass.
The other qualifying findings concern file download access control, Cloud Code execution limits, Cloud Code context prototype pollution, and browser security headers.
